name: Mike Doyle Golf Academy
about: Mike Doyle Golf Academy is an indoor golf training facility located at GolfX in Franklin, MA. It utilizes state-of-the-art technology, including launch monitors and virtual golf simulators, to provide accurate ball data for lessons and practice. The academy offers a variety of programs including private instruction, beginner group lessons, junior golf programs, and evening clinics for players of all skill levels. Operating year-round, it is positioned as a true New England Golf School experience with convenient access from Providence and Boston.
why_golfers_like_it: Access to state-of-the-art launch monitors and virtual golf simulators for accurate data
Structured programs led by a USGTF-certified teaching professional
Year-round indoor facility providing a consistent learning environment
Flexible lesson packages suitable for various skill levels and goals

food_and_drink.notes: No food service mentioned. BYOB is likely allowed but not explicitly stated.
No alcohol service mentioned. BYOB is likely allowed but not explicitly stated.

simulator_hardware.notes: Website mentions 'latest state of the art technology launch monitors' but does not specify the brand.

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: Yes, students are expected to bring their own golf clubs for lessons and practice sessions.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drink?
faq.answer: The website does not explicitly state a policy on outside food and drink. It's recommended to contact the academy directly for clarification.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: The website does not provide information on the time required to complete 18 holes on the simulator. This would depend on the software and number of players in a group.

faq.question: Is the facility kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, the academy offers junior golf programs and is suitable for younger players learning the game.
